title: Should We Sunset Anchor Classic?
bluf: Sunset Anchor Classic with a 12-month customer migration runway ending March 2028.
thesis: Anchor Classic's revenue is shrinking while it consumes an outsized share of engineering time; sunsetting it with a funded migration runway lets the company focus on Anchor Cloud without abandoning existing customers.

nodes.label: Decision
nodes.title: Sunset Anchor Classic
nodes.summary: Whether to sunset the legacy on-premise product Anchor Classic, and on what timeline.
nodes.question: Should Anchor Classic be sunset, and if so, on what timeline?

sourceRefs.quote: should Anchor Classic be sunset, and if so, on what timeline?

nodes.label: Option A
nodes.title: Immediate sunset, no migration support
nodes.summary: Stop selling Anchor Classic today and end support within 90 days.

sourceRefs.quote: Stop selling Anchor Classic today and end support within 90 days.

nodes.label: Option B
nodes.title: Sunset with a 12-month migration runway
nodes.summary: Stop selling Anchor Classic today, support existing customers through March 2028, and fund a dedicated migration team. This is the recommended option.

sourceRefs.quote: Stop selling Anchor Classic today, commit to supporting existing customers through March 2028, and fund a dedicated migration team to move customers onto Anchor Cloud.

nodes.label: Option C
nodes.title: Keep maintaining Anchor Classic indefinitely
nodes.summary: Continue selling and supporting both Anchor Classic and Anchor Cloud.

sourceRefs.quote: Continue selling and supporting both products.

nodes.title: Anchor Classic is no longer defensible to maintain
nodes.summary: Declining revenue plus disproportionate engineering cost mean Anchor Classic's current form is no longer defensible.

sourceRefs.quote: The declining revenue and disproportionate engineering cost of Anchor Classic mean continuing to maintain it in its current form is no longer defensible.

nodes.title: Private-VPC removes the original reason for on-premise
nodes.summary: The compliance requirements that originally justified on-premise deployment are now met by Anchor Cloud's private-VPC option.

sourceRefs.quote: The compliance requirements that justified on-premise deployment in 2019 are now met by the new private-VPC deployment option in Anchor Cloud, which removes the original reason many customers chose Anchor Classic.

nodes.title: Six quarters of revenue decline
nodes.summary: Anchor Classic ARR fell from $2.1M to $1.4M over six consecutive quarters.

sourceRefs.quote: Anchor Classic revenue has declined for six consecutive quarters, from $2.1M ARR in Q1 2026 to $1.4M ARR in Q4 2026.

nodes.title: Disproportionate engineering cost
nodes.summary: The on-premise stack consumes 31% of sprint capacity while generating only 8% of revenue.

sourceRefs.quote: Engineering time-tracking shows the on-premise stack consumes 31% of sprint capacity despite generating 8% of revenue.

nodes.title: Private-VPC reaches general availability in time
nodes.summary: Assumes the Anchor Cloud private-VPC option, currently in beta, reaches general availability before the migration runway begins in earnest.

sourceRefs.quote: We are assuming the private-VPC deployment option in Anchor Cloud, currently in beta, will be generally available before the migration runway begins in earnest.

nodes.title: Compliance customers accept private-VPC
nodes.summary: Assumes the twenty-three compliance-driven customers will accept private-VPC as a substitute for fully on-premise deployment; not yet validated with all of them.

sourceRefs.quote: We are assuming the twenty-three compliance-driven customers will accept private-VPC as a substitute for fully on-premise deployment; we have not validated this with all of them yet.

nodes.label: R1
nodes.title: Customer churn risk
nodes.summary: Largest healthcare accounts may leave rather than migrate if private-VPC does not meet their compliance needs.

sourceRefs.quote: some of our largest healthcare accounts may leave rather than migrate, especially if the private-VPC option does not meet their compliance needs.

nodes.label: R2
nodes.title: Competitor opportunity risk
nodes.summary: A sunset announcement gives competitors an opening to court the Anchor Classic customer base during the migration window.

sourceRefs.quote: a sunset announcement gives competitors an opening to court our Anchor Classic customer base during the migration window.

nodes.title: Engineering time reclaimed
nodes.summary: Option A reclaims engineering time fastest but preserves the least customer trust.

sourceRefs.quote: Option A reclaims engineering time fastest but preserves the least customer trust.

nodes.title: Customer trust preserved
nodes.summary: Option C preserves the most customer trust but reclaims no engineering time.

sourceRefs.quote: Option C preserves the most trust but reclaims no engineering time.

nodes.title: Finalize private-VPC GA date
nodes.summary: Sarah Kim will finalize the private-VPC general-availability date.

sourceRefs.quote: Sarah Kim (VP Engineering) will finalize the private-VPC general-availability date by March 20, 2027.

nodes.title: Call all Anchor Classic customers
nodes.summary: Marcus Webb will personally call all thirty-one Anchor Classic customers before the sunset announcement.

sourceRefs.quote: Marcus Webb (Head of Customer Success) will personally call all thirty-one Anchor Classic customers before the sunset announcement, starting March 10, 2027.

nodes.title: Draft migration guide
nodes.summary: Priya Nair will draft the customer-facing migration guide.

sourceRefs.quote: Priya Nair (Head of Product) will draft the customer-facing migration guide by April 1, 2027.

nodes.title: Discount for early migrators?
nodes.summary: Should we offer a discount on Anchor Cloud pricing to Anchor Classic customers who migrate before the runway ends?

sourceRefs.quote: Should we offer a discount on Anchor Cloud pricing to Anchor Classic customers who migrate before the runway ends?

nodes.title: Contingency for compliance rejections
nodes.summary: What is the contingency plan if more than five of the twenty-three compliance-driven customers reject the private-VPC option?

sourceRefs.quote: What is our contingency plan if more than five of the twenty-three compliance-driven customers reject the private-VPC option?

nodes.title: Sarah Kim
nodes.summary: VP Engineering; owns the private-VPC general-availability timeline.

sourceRefs.quote: Sarah Kim (VP Engineering)

nodes.title: Marcus Webb
nodes.summary: Head of Customer Success; owns customer outreach for the sunset.

sourceRefs.quote: Marcus Webb (Head of Customer Success)

nodes.title: Priya Nair
nodes.summary: Head of Product; owns the customer-facing migration guide.

sourceRefs.quote: Priya Nair (Head of Product)
